article_items.summary: The mix is what the model sees at training time with noise augmentation enabled. vLLM ships a closed set of multimodal architectures out of the box ( Qwen2-Audio , LLaVA , Phi-4-MM , a couple more). The practical impact: When the user writes a prompt with one <|AUDIO|> , the processor expands it into <|start_of_audio|> + 375× <|AUDIO|> + <|start_of_audio|> , and marks each of those 375 tokens as "embedding will be supplied externally" via PromptUpdateDetails.select_token_id(..., embed_token_id=audio_token_id) . Where the 2.1× comes from. Watch next: The plugin ( vllm_borealis ) sits next to the weights in the HF model repo. Where the 2.1× comes from.

article_items.title: What ClickUp's mass layoff tells us about the future of work | TechCrunch
article_items.description: The nine-year-old startup is replacing hundreds of employees with thousands of AI agents.
article_items.excerpt: The nine-year-old startup is replacing hundreds of employees with thousands of AI agents.
article_items.summary: Last Thursday, Evans announced on X that the company, which was last valued in 2021 at $4 billion , had laid off 22% of its workforce yet characterized that reduction as not a cost-cutting measure, but rather a radical embrace of AI that will propel the company to the next level. ClickUp recently introduced roughly 3,000 internal AI agents to handle a wide range of complex tasks on behalf of its employees, according to a Fortune article published several days ago. The practical impact: Instead of performing the work themselves, staff members are now expected to direct these agents and ultimately review the output to ensure it meets the company’s standards. “Most savings from this change will flow directly back into the people who stay. Watch next: More from TechCrunch What ClickUp’s mass layoff tells us about the future of work AI’s biggest champions have argued for some time that the technology will usher in an era of unprecedented productivity gains, richly rewarding workers who harness it while displacing those who don’t. The first StrictlyVC...

That efficiency is apparently paying off: Polsia just raised $30 million at a $250 million valuation.
The nine-year-old startup is replacing hundreds of employees with thousands of AI agents.
Evans told TechCrunch via email that the startup is indeed seeing productivity gains from AI agents.
Instead of performing the work themselves, staff members are now expected to direct these agents and ultimately review the output to ensure it meets the company’s standards.
One extreme example of a high-profile startup using AI automation to the max already exists.
Evans’s goal, according to his X post, is for AI to turbocharge ClickUp into a “100x org.” ClickUp is not alone in its hope that AI agents will provide massive productivity gains.
Zeb Evans, CEO of the collaboration software startup ClickUp, claims that this shift is imminent.
You can contact or verify outreach from Marina by emailing marina.temkin@techcrunch.com or via encrypted message at +1 347-683-3909 on Signal.
In fact, according to a recent Gartner survey, about 80% of companies using autonomous tech have cut jobs.
